articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

articles.headline: Dortmund captain Emre Can out for season with torn ACL
articles.description: Borussia Dortmund captain Emre Can will miss the rest of the season with a torn ACL in his left knee.

images.name: Can Dortmund torn ACL_260228 [1296x729]
images.caption: Emre Can leaves the field during Dortmund's game against Bayern Munich after sustaining an injury.
images.alt: Emre Can leaves the field during Dortmund's game against Bayern Munich after sustaining an injury.

images.name: Bayern Munich wins 3-2 thriller over Dortmund
images.caption: Bayern Munich gets a late goal from Joshua Kimmich to beat Borussia Dortmund 3-2 in Der Klassiker.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Lukas Kübler (SC Freiburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Penalty saved. Florian Wirtz (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) right footed shot saved in the bottom left corner.
Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 1, SC Freiburg 0. Patrik Schick (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from outside the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Florian Wirtz.

Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, SC Freiburg 0. Florian Wirtz (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) right footed shot from the left side of the six yard box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Exequiel Palacios with a through ball.
Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 2, SC Freiburg 1. Vincenzo Grifo (SC Freiburg) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Michael Gregoritsch.
Substitution, SC Freiburg. Merlin Röhl replaces Ritsu Doan.
Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 3, SC Freiburg 1. Patrik Schick (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) header from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Florian Wirtz with a cross.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Martin Terrier replaces Nathan Tella.

Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 4, SC Freiburg 1. Patrik Schick (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Florian Wirtz following a fast break.
Substitution, SC Freiburg. Junior Adamu replaces Michael Gregoritsch.
Substitution, SC Freiburg. Kiliann Sildillia replaces Patrick Osterhage.
Substitution, SC Freiburg. Max Rosenfelder replaces Lukas Kübler.
Goal! Bayer 04 Leverkusen 5, SC Freiburg 1. Patrik Schick (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) header from very close range to the high centre of the goal. Assisted by Álex Grimaldo with a cross following a corner.
Patrik Schick (Bayer 04 Leverkusen) is shown the yellow card for excessive celebration.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Nordi Mukiele replaces Álex Grimaldo.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Aleix García replaces Patrik Schick.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Arthur replaces Jeremie Frimpong.
Substitution, Bayer 04 Leverkusen. Jeanuël Belocian replaces Piero Hincapié.
Substitution, SC Freiburg. Nicolas Höfler replaces Maximilian Eggestein.
Merlin Röhl (SC Freiburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.

article.headline: Leverkusen's 5-1 win over Freiburg tougher than it seemed, says Alonso
article.description: Bayer Leverkusen manager Xabi Alonso praised his team's determination after a commanding 5-1 win over Freiburg on Saturday, stressing the contest was more difficult than the scoreline suggested.

article.story: Dec 21 (Reuters) - Bayer Leverkusen manager Xabi Alonso praised his team's determination after a commanding 5-1 win over Freiburg on Saturday, stressing the contest was more difficult than the scoreline suggested. Leverkusen seemingly had little trouble cruising past Freiburg at home in the Bundesliga clash, with Patrik Schick scoring four times and Florian Wirtz adding another. "The result may have been spectacular and Patrik scored four goals but the game was not as easy as it seemed. We had a good opponent and we had to prepare our actions intelligently," Alonso told reporters. He praised Schick's impressive performance, noting it was the result of the team's collective effort. "When a striker scores four goals you can't complain. Patrik also does a lot of work off the ball and when he does that well, that's why he scores the goal," he said. "He helps the team and they make the chances for him." The win puts second-placed Leverkusen on 32 points, four off Bayern Munich who thrashed RB Leipzig 5-1 on Friday. Asked about the significance of the result ahead of the new year, Alonso emphasised it was a much-needed one. "It was a very important victory for us today. We wanted to continue our series and go into the break with a good feeling. We managed that. We will continue to attack in the new year," he said. "That gives everyone a good feeling. We have big ambitions to continue fighting for our goals in 2025 and want to achieve a lot together." Leverkusen will continue their campaign at Borussia Dortmund on Jan. 10. (Reporting by Tommy Lund in Gdansk; Editing by Ken Ferris)
